name: Pickled Carrot, Fennel, and Radish Relish
recipeInstructions:
1. Using a mandoline or sharp knife, cut carrots into short 1/8-inch thick julienne strips.
2. Cut the bell pepper into short 1/8-inch thick julienne strips using a sharp knife.
3. Steam the carrot strips in a steamer set over simmering water for 5 to 6 minutes, or until crisp-tender. Transfer to a bowl.
4. Steam the bell pepper for 1 to 2 minutes, or until crisp-tender, then add it to the carrot mixture.
5. Halve the fennel bulb lengthwise and remove the core. Cut the fennel into short 1/8-inch thick julienne strips.
6. Halve the onion lengthwise and cut into short 1/8-inch thick julienne strips.
7. Add the fennel and onion to the carrot mixture.
8. In a saucepan, bring the remaining ingredients (cider vinegar, sugar, vegetable oil, ketchup, mustard seeds, salt, and garlic) to a boil, stirring to dissolve the sugar.
9. Pour the boiling mixture over the vegetables and marinate them covered in the refrigerator for at least 1 hour, up to 1 week.
10. Serve the relish chilled or at room temperature with poultry, veal, pork, or lamb.
